content.text: Sunan Ibn Mājah is a collection of hadīth compiled by Imām Muḥammad bin Yazīd Ibn Mājah al-Qazvīnī (raḥimahullāh). It is widely regarded as the sixth of the six canonical collections of hadith (Kutub as-Sittah) and contains 4,341 hadīth arranged in 37 books.

Abū `Abdullāh Muḥammad bin Yazīd bin `Abdullāh ar-Rab`ī al-Qazvīnī, known as Ibn Mājah, was born in 209 AH in Qazvin (Iran). He belonged to the Rab`i tribe. According to some scholars, Mājah was his mother's name, while others considered it to be his father's nickname.

Ibn Mājah began studying Hadith in Qazvin and later travelled extensively through Khurasan, Iraq, Hijaz, Egypt, Sham, Makkah, Madinah, Baghdad, Damascus, Homs, Isfahan, Ashkelon and Nishapur, learning from leading scholars of Hadith.

He studied under many distinguished scholars in Makkah, Madinah and Qazvin, including Hafiz Ibn Mus'ab az-Zubairi, Ahmad bin Abi Bakr al-'Awfi, Ibrahim bin al-Mundhir, Hasan bin 'Ali al-Khilal, Zubair bin Bakkar, Salamah bin Shabib, Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaibah and many others.

Among his well-known students were 'Ali bin 'Abdullah al-Falani, Ibrahim bin Dinar al-Jarshi, Ahmad bin Ibrahim al-Qazwini, Hafiz Abu Ya'la al-Khalili and Abu 'Amr Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Hakim al-Madani al-Isfahani.

Imam Ibn Mājah was recognized as a leading Hadith scholar, Qur'an commentator and historian. Scholars such as Imam adh-Dhahabi, Abu Ya'la al-Khalili and Allamah Sindi praised his knowledge, trustworthiness, memorization and scholarship.
His Tafsir and Tarikh were highly praised by scholars but are no longer extant.
Imam Abu 'Abdullah Muhammad Ibn Mājah passed away on Monday, 22 Ramadan 273 AH at the age of 64.

Sunan Ibn Mājah covers Fiqh, Aqidah, dreams, tribulations and asceticism. It contains 37 books, 1,560 chapters and 4,341 hadith, including 1,339 additional narrations (Zawa'id) not found in the other five major Hadith collections.

Although Ibn Mājah did not state formal conditions for his compilation, the work contains more hadith than the other five collections without repetition and includes a larger number of weak narrations because of his objective of gathering evidences for Fiqh issues.
Special Characteristics of Sunan Ibn Majah:

items: Contains 482 authentic hadith not found in the other five books.
Includes 3,002 hadith shared with other collections through different chains of narration.
Contains 1,339 unique Zawa'id narrations that elevated it to the sixth position among the Kutub as-Sittah.
